_meta.author: Claude (va-legislature-refine overnight autopilot)
_meta.note: VA State Senate batch — Districts 11-15 (state tier). Creigh Deeds (SD-11, D), Glen Sturtevant (SD-12, R), Lashrecse Aird (SD-13, D), Lamont Bagby (SD-14, D), Michael Jones (SD-15, D). Evidence-first finalization from VPAP/LIS roll calls, ReproRising 2025 scorecard, Giffords/Everytown endorsements, iVoterGuide, and reputable VA press (Cardinal News, Virginia Mercury, VPM). KEY VERIFIED FACTS: (1) On HJ3 (same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment, Senate 26-13, 01/16/2026) the ONLY 5 GOP crossovers were DeSteph, Diggs, Durant, Jordan, Suetterlein — Sturtevant voted NO, did NOT cross. (2) Aird is a CO-patron of SJ247 (repro-freedom amendment), NOT chief patron (chief patron = Boysko); scored accordingly. (3) Michael Jones is a Christian pastor (Village of Faith Ministries) but his LIS-verified recorded votes are uniformly progressive on life, marriage, guns — pastoral identity did NOT produce a conservative voting record; scored on votes, not identity. The 4 Democrats land near 0/100; Sturtevant scores high (consistent conservative record, only null cells where no public evidence exists).

profile.background: VA State Senator since 2001 (House of Delegates 1992-2001); attorney, former Bath County Commonwealth's Attorney; 2005 AG nominee and 2009 Democratic gubernatorial nominee. SD-11 is a sprawling central/western VA district (Bath, Alleghany, Charlottesville/Albemarle area) after redistricting from the old SD-25. Virginia's leading mental-health-reform legislator following his son's 2013 death.

creigh-deeds.notes_set: VA State Senator, SD-11 (sitting since 2001). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Long-tenured mainstream Senate Democrat. Pro-abortion-rights HARD record: named 2023 'Reproductive Freedom Champion' by ReproRising (formerly NARAL), endorsed by the group, in the Democratic majority advancing the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (SJ247/HJ1). Marriage: in the majority advancing the same-sex-marriage amendment (2025 & 2026 references) and publicly opposed Youngkin's transgender-student model policy. Opposed school choice/ESAs ('nonstarter… defund public schools') and voter ID (urged a 2012 veto). Economic: backs the $15 minimum wage. Public justice: sponsored bail reform (SB1266) and voted to abolish the death penalty (2021). Guns: sponsored an assault-weapons ban (SB1382), backed universal background checks/safe-storage; GIFFORDS-endorsed. CROSS-CURRENT (historical, noted not scored): in 2009 voted against in-state tuition/benefits for undocumented immigrants and for English-as-official-language — restrictionist votes atypical of his caucus, but 2009-era and not mapping to the current border cells. Several amendment roll-call lines are party-line-majority inferences (VPAP blocked automated read) but his alignment is independently corroborated by HARD endorsements/statements. No conservative deviations on the scored cells.

0.note: Named ReproRising 'Reproductive Freedom Champion'; in the Democratic majority advancing the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (SJ247/HJ1) — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Pro-abortion-rights record (ReproRising/NARAL endorsed) — opposite of abortion abolition.

0.note: In the Democratic majority advancing the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment (Senate 24-15 in 2025, 26-13 in 2026) — does not affirm marriage as one man and one woman.

1.note: Supported codifying same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution.

2.note: Publicly opposed Gov. Youngkin's transgender-student model policy ('I do not support the governor's policy') — does not reject transgender ideology.

4.note: Opposed the model policy restricting LGBTQ-identifying students in schools; does not oppose LGBTQ promotion in school policy.

0.note: Called the school-choice/ESA bill a 'nonstarter' that would 'defund public schools'; opposes universal school choice.

1.note: Opposed Youngkin's parental-notification model policy on transgender students; frames it as a school-board matter rather than a state parental-consent mandate.

2.note: Backs raising the minimum wage to $15 and criticized Youngkin's veto — aligned with expanded-spending Democratic budget priorities.

1.note: Publicly opposed Virginia's voter-ID law and urged Gov. McDonnell to veto it (2012).
Sponsored bail reform (SB1266) to end presumptions against bail — opposite of opposing cashless/soft-on-crime bail policy.

2.note: Voted to abolish Virginia's death penalty (2021) and favors reducing mandatory minimums — leniency on proportional punishment.

1.note: Sponsored an assault-weapons ban (SB1382) and backed universal background checks/safe-storage; GIFFORDS-endorsed — supports red-flag/magazine/assault-weapon restrictions.

profile.religion: No publicly documented denomination (B.A. from The Catholic University of America is an education credential, not a stated church affiliation — not inferred)
profile.background: VA State Senator for SD-12 (Colonial Heights + parts of Chesterfield County, Richmond suburbs); attorney and former prosecutor (Rawls Law Group). Served the old SD-10 2016-2020, lost in 2019, returned to the Senate in 2024 after winning the 2023 primary and general. He and his wife are raising four adopted children.

glen-sturtevant.notes_set: VA State Senator, SD-12 (returned to the Senate 2024; prior service 2016-2020). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Consistent Christian-conservative record. Life: self-described '100% pro-life,' graded F by ReproRising 2025, opposed the reproductive-freedom amendment with the GOP bloc. Marriage (VERIFIED, the key cell): voted NO on HJ3, the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment (Senate 26-13, 01/16/2026) — the ONLY 5 Republican crossovers were DeSteph, Diggs, Durant, Jordan, and Suetterlein; Sturtevant was in the 13-member NO bloc, did NOT cross. Family: backs school choice ('money follows the child') and sponsored parental content-opt-out legislation. Elections: supports restoring photo voter ID. Immigration: restrictionist; sponsored an ICE-transfer bill for incarcerated persons. Guns: opposed the 2026 mandatory safe-storage bill and argued to repeal the red-flag law. ONE DOCUMENTED RECORD DEVIATION (noted, not scored): voted FOR red-flag legislation in 2019 but now favors repeal — his current position is opposition, which is what's scored. Cells with no public evidence (christian liberty, federal overreach, CBDC/ESG, death-penalty, a numeric Giffords grade) are left null, NOT scored from party.

0.note: Self-described '100% pro-life' who 'has voted against every bill that does not uphold the right to life for the unborn'; graded F by ReproRising 2025 and opposed the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment.

Voted NO on HJ3, the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment (Senate 26-13, 01/16/2026); the only 5 GOP crossovers were DeSteph/Diggs/Durant/Jordan/Suetterlein — Sturtevant was in the NO bloc, affirming one-man/one-woman marriage.

1.note: Voted NO on codifying same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution — opposes same-sex marriage in law.

0.note: Backs school choice — per-pupil funding should follow the child to the parents' chosen school.

2.note: Sponsored parental-rights legislation letting parents opt children out of certain educational content.

1.note: Supports restoring photo voter-ID requirements to vote.

2.note: Sponsored legislation for ICE transfer of certain incarcerated persons and decries the state being 'flooded with illegal immigrants' — pro-enforcement, anti-sanctuary posture.

1.note: Opposed the 2026 mandatory safe-storage bill and argued to repeal Virginia's red-flag (substantial-risk order) law on due-process grounds.

profile.religion: Reported Baptist (member, Tabernacle Baptist Church, Petersburg) per VoteSmart/aggregators — secondary-sourced, not a primary self-statement
profile.background: VA State Senator for SD-13 since Jan 2024 (former Delegate 2016-2022). District covers Petersburg, Surry, and southside Richmond-area Southside Virginia. Higher-education administrator (Richard Bland College); Senate Democratic Caucus Vice Chair (2026).

lashrecse-aird.notes_set: VA State Senator, SD-13 (sitting since Jan 2024; former Delegate). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Uniform progressive record. Life: CO-patron of SJ247 (the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment — NOTE: chief patron is Boysko, not Aird, contrary to a common misstatement); ReproRising A+ 'Champion'; NARAL-endorsed; sits on the Planned Parenthood Virginia League board (HARD abortion-industry tie); iVoterGuide reports she 'strongly disagrees' that life begins at conception. Marriage: voted YES to advance HJ3, the same-sex-marriage amendment (Senate 26-13, 01/16/2026). Economic: voted to repeal right-to-work; aligned with the $15 minimum wage. Elections: opposes a photo-ID requirement to vote. Immigration: iVoterGuide reports she 'strongly disagrees' with building a border wall. Guns: GIFFORDS- and Everytown-endorsed; credited with the red-flag law and expanded background checks. NO cross-party deviations found. christian_liberty and refuse_federal_overreach returned no evidence → left null.

0.note: iVoterGuide reports she 'strongly disagrees' that human life begins at conception; co-patron of the reproductive-freedom amendment; ReproRising A+ 'Champion.'

1.note: Co-patron of SJ247, the constitutional amendment enshrining a fundamental right to reproductive freedom — opposite of abortion abolition.

4.note: Sits on the Planned Parenthood Virginia League board and is NARAL-endorsed — documented abortion-industry backing.

0.note: Voted YES to advance HJ3, the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment (Senate 26-13, 01/16/2026).

1.note: Supported codifying same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution.

2.note: Voted to repeal right-to-work and aligned with the $15 minimum wage / Medicaid expansion — expanded-spending priorities.

1.note: Public position that people should be able to vote without photo ID — opposes a photo-voter-ID requirement.

0.note: iVoterGuide reports she 'strongly disagrees' with building a border wall — opposes a completed physical border barrier.

1.note: GIFFORDS credits her with Virginia's red-flag (extreme-risk protection order) law and expanding background checks to all gun sales — supports red-flag/background-check restrictions.

profile.religion: No publicly documented personal church affiliation (has sponsored commending resolutions for several Richmond-area Black congregations, but no source states his own membership)
profile.background: VA State Senator for SD-14 (parts of Henrico County and Richmond City) since a March 2023 special election; former Delegate 2015-2023 and former Henrico County School Board member. Former Henrico High teacher/administrator; chairs the Virginia Legislative Black Caucus and was elected Democratic Party of Virginia chair in 2025.

lamont-bagby.notes_set: VA State Senator, SD-14 (sitting since 2023; former Delegate). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Uniform progressive record (CPAC lifetime rating 12%). Life: voted YEA on SJ247 (reproductive-freedom amendment, named in the 21-Yea roll call). Marriage: in the Democratic bloc advancing the same-sex-marriage amendment (SJ249 2025, HJ3 2026). Economic: party-line YES on the $15 minimum wage; VA League of Conservation Voters-endorsed. Elections: lead figure in the 2020 Democratic election-law overhaul that REPEALED photo voter ID, created no-excuse early voting and same-day/automatic registration; patron of Sunday-voting legislation; voted for the felon-voting-rights amendment. Immigration: voted with the majority for the 2026 package limiting ICE cooperation (SB351/SB352). Guns: GIFFORDS-endorsed (one-handgun-a-month, red-flag, background checks). NO cross-party deviations found. christian_liberty, refuse_federal_overreach, school-choice and public_justice cells had no specific cited vote → left null rather than inferred.

0.note: Voted YEA on SJ247, the 'fundamental right to reproductive freedom' constitutional amendment (Senate 21-19, 01/21/2025) — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Voted for the reproductive-freedom amendment — opposite of abortion abolition.

0.note: In the Democratic majority advancing the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment (SJ249 24-15 in 2025; HJ3 26-13 in 2026).

1.note: Supported codifying same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution.

2.note: Part of the Democratic majority passing the $15 minimum wage; CPAC lifetime rating 12% — consistently pro-spending economic votes.

1.note: Lead figure in the 2020 overhaul that REPEALED Virginia's photo-ID requirement — opposite of supporting photo voter ID.

2.note: Helped create no-excuse early voting and same-day registration — expanded beyond single-day in-person voting.

3.note: Helped enact no-excuse absentee/early voting — opposite of opposing mass mail-in/expanded absentee voting.

2.note: Voted with the Democratic majority for the 2026 package limiting ICE cooperation (SB351/SB352) — supports limits on immigration enforcement, opposite of opposing sanctuary policy.

1.note: GIFFORDS credits him with reinstating one-handgun-a-month, red-flag (extreme-risk) laws, and tighter background checks — supports red-flag/registration-type restrictions.

profile.religion: Christian; founding Senior Pastor of Village of Faith Ministries (non-denominational, Richmond area). M.Div., Samuel DeWitt Proctor School of Theology (Virginia Union); D.Min., McCormick Theological Seminary
profile.background: VA State Senator for SD-15 (parts of Richmond City and Chesterfield County) since Jan 2026; former Delegate HD-77 (2024-2026) and Richmond City Council member/President (2016-2023). Full-time pastor and Democrat; won the Jan 2026 special election for the seat vacated when Ghazala Hashmi became Lt. Governor.

michael-jones.notes_set: VA State Senator, SD-15 (sitting since Jan 2026; former Delegate HD-77; former Richmond City Council President). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] HONEST KEY FINDING: Jones is a Christian pastor (Village of Faith Ministries), but his LIS-verified recorded votes are uniformly progressive — pastoral identity did NOT produce a socially conservative voting record, and he is scored on his votes, not his vocation. Life: co-patron + YES on the reproductive-freedom amendment (HJ1/SJ247 2025; HB781/SB449 2026); ReproRising A+. Marriage: YES on HJ9 (2025) repealing the one-man/one-woman definition and YES on HB612/SB311 (2026). Economic: YES on multiple minimum-wage bills and paid family leave. Elections: YES on the felon-voting-rights amendment (HJ2) and on expanding in-person absentee voting/registration. Public justice: introduced bills banning tear gas/rubber bullets/chokeholds and voted for marijuana-offense resentencing. Guns: chief patron of an auto-sears ban (HB22) and YES on the assault-firearms/high-capacity-magazine ban (HB1607); GIFFORDS-endorsed. Across ~2,094 recorded 2025 House votes he cast only 3 idiosyncratic NO votes, none a conservative break. christian_liberty (a church-property tax-exemption YES does not map to the symbol/prayer/conscience cells), refuse_federal_overreach, school choice, and sanctuary cells left null.

0.note: Co-patron and YES vote on HJ1 (2025), the 'fundamental right to reproductive freedom' amendment; ReproRising A+ — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Voted for the reproductive-freedom amendment (and the 2026 Senate companions HB781/SB449) — opposite of abortion abolition.

0.note: Voted YES on HJ9 (2025) repealing the one-man/one-woman marriage definition and on the 2026 Senate companions (HB612/SB311) — does not affirm one-man/one-woman marriage.

1.note: Voted to codify same-sex marriage as a fundamental right in the Virginia Constitution.

2.note: Voted YES on multiple minimum-wage bills (HB1928, HB2561, HB1625) and paid family/medical leave (HB2531) — expanded-spending economic priorities.
Voted YES on HB2056 (2025) expanding in-person absentee voting days/hours — expanded beyond single-day in-person voting.

3.note: Voted to expand absentee voting and voter registration — opposite of opposing mass mail-in/expanded absentee voting.

1.note: Voted for marijuana-offense sentence-modification/resentencing (HB2555) — leniency consistent with criminal-justice-reform, opposite of opposing soft-on-crime policy.
Chief patron of an auto-sears ban (HB22) and YES on the assault-firearms/high-capacity-magazine ban (HB1607); GIFFORDS-endorsed — supports assault-weapon/magazine restrictions.
